shots.narrative_description: A founder named Vivek introduces his company Edso and explains the concept of giving AI language models access to video editing tools.
An introduction to Edso, showing the company logo and branding as the speaker explains the initial concept behind creating an AI-powered video editing tool.
A person discusses the founding concept of Edso, explaining the idea of giving LLMs access to editing tools to enable them to edit videos.
A static logo screen for EDSO is displayed with an orange icon and white text on a black background, with a small orange button below.
A presenter introduces the problem that motivated them to build a prototype solution.
A speaker introduces and prepares to demonstrate their first prototype built to solve a stated question.
A person begins to demonstrate a built project showing the EDSO interface with an audio player.
A video player interface appears and moves upward against a black background, with loading and playback controls visible.
A person uploads sample video and audio files to a media management interface for analysis.
A person explains that uploaded content gets analyzed during the upload process, with minimal interface changes visible.
A static screen displaying a video player interface with a light gray and black split view, showing minimal activity in the bottom left corner.
A person discusses their attempt to edit and create comedic content through commentary.
A person is editing and attempting to add a comedic comment to a video or text.
A speaker discusses a technical process where analysis passes through to an LLM with editing prompting, while a hand holds a yellow card or notebook with a beaded necklace.
A person holds a yellow notebook with a beaded necklace while explaining that analysis is passed through to an LLM with editing prompting.
A presenter explains how an LLM analyzes data and determines editing commands using prompting.
A video editing interface displays a yellow notebook and necklace while demonstrating an AI-powered editing workflow with timeline controls visible.
A demonstration shows the final edited output with yellow envelope imagery, featuring added text, audio, and video elements in a timeline interface.
A screen recording demonstrates adding text, audio, and video to a project, showing a yellow notebook with a necklace in the preview window and a timeline at the bottom.
A video player interface appears over a still image showing a yellow envelope with a necklace, with playback controls visible in the center.

transcript.text: Hey, thanks for your time. My name is Vivek and I'm the founder of Edso. So the initial thought behind, uh, starting Edso was say, if you could actually give LLM, uh, all the access to editing tools, um, and making them understand videos, can they actually edit? That was the question I started with and I've started building up a prototype to solve that. And this is my first prototype. Uh, I'll just quickly demo what I've built. So I'm just uploading a few sample videos and audios. Um, so they get analyzed at upload. And I'm trying to, uh, edit by giving a comment and make a comedy read. So this is where there's a, uh, the analysis passed through to LLM, uh, along with some editing prompting and all. And LLM decides what editing commands have to be done. And those commands get executed. So you can see the final output after the LLM edits. This is how it looks. It also adds text, audios, and video. Thank you.

segments.text: My name is Vivek and I'm the founder of Edso.
So the initial thought behind, uh, starting Edso was say, if you could actually give LLM, uh, all the access to editing tools, um, and making them understand videos, can they actually edit?
That was the question I started with and I've started building up a prototype to solve that.
And this is my first prototype.
Uh, I'll just quickly demo what I've built.
So I'm just uploading a few sample videos and audios.
Um, so they get analyzed at upload.
And I'm trying to, uh, edit by giving a comment and make a comedy read.
So this is where there's a, uh, the analysis passed through to LLM, uh, along with some editing prompting and all.
And LLM decides what editing commands have to be done.
So you can see the final output after the LLM edits.
It also adds text, audios, and video.
